markets.question: Will Colorado Rapids win the 2026 MLS Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLS Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLS Cup per the rules of MLS (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF leads trader consensus for the 2026 MLS Cup at 17.5% implied probability, buoyed by Lionel Messi's ongoing scoring prowess—including Goal of the Matchday 11—and their potent attack despite a third-place Eastern Conference standing (19 points from 11 matches). LAFC (11.5%) and Vancouver Whitecaps FC (10.1%) trail closely, reflecting the latter's Western Conference dominance (25 points, +20 goal difference) via a league-high 26 goals and Vancouver's eight wins in 10 games, while LAFC's solid third-place form (21 points) underscores defensive strength. This tight race stems from MLS parity early in the 34-match regular season, with playoffs' single-elimination format favoring momentum, home advantage, and star power over Supporters' Shield leaders like San Jose Earthquakes (28 points), keeping the field wide open amid unproven postseason pedigrees.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Konyaspor and Fenerbahçe SK.

eventMetadata.context_description: Konyaspor's shocking 1-0 extra-time victory over Fenerbahçe in the Turkish Cup quarterfinal on April 21 at home has dramatically leveled trader sentiment for this Super Lig clash, exposing defensive lapses in the visitors despite their second-place table position behind Galatasaray. Hosting at Medaş Konya Büyükşehir Stadyumu, Konyaspor leverages strong home form against top sides, where they've secured draws and upsets, while Fenerbahçe's historical head-to-head dominance (18 wins to 7) is tempered by recent vulnerabilities amid title pressure and end-of-season fatigue. Minor injuries like Konyaspor's Yhoan Andzouana (muscle) sideline key depth, but full-strength lineups featuring Fener's Ederson, Skriniar, and Kanté keep probabilities tightly bunched around 50%, with draw consensus reflecting a gritty, low-scoring stalemate potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices PSOE-A at 30-32 seats (41%) as the leading outcome for the May 17 Andalusian regional election to the 109-seat Parliament, reflecting the upper range of recent April polls projecting 26-34 seats (averaging 27-28 from CIS, Sigma Dos, GAD3, and others). This positioning stems from PSOE-A's modest recovery from 2025 lows under leader María Jesús Montero, bolstered by fallout from the PP-led government's October 2025 breast cancer screening scandal—exposing 2,000+ delayed diagnoses, record manipulation, and Health Minister resignation—which has eroded the incumbent's "good manager" image without collapsing their projected 53-58 seats. Vox's 13-20 seat gains and left fragmentation (Por Andalucía, Adelante Andalucía) constrain PSOE upside, while 24-26 seats (29.5%) captures downside risk in this former socialist stronghold amid national PSOE headwinds. Final campaign pushes and turnout could tip ranges before resolution.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. Chris Oladokun's 24.9% reflects his entrenched practice squad role and offseason buzz as a surprising contender over Fields, per mid-April reports, while veterans Joe Flacco (18.8%) and Gardner Minshew (8.1%) linger as free-agent fallback options amid roster depth including draft pick Garrett Nussmeier.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's May 2 announcement of withdrawing 5,000 US troops from Germany within 6-12 months—fulfilling a Trump campaign pledge amid European allies' shortfalls on defense spending targets—has alarmed NATO partners and sparked speculation but stops short of formal withdrawal under Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty, which requires one year's notice of denunciation. Trader consensus assigns low implied probabilities (around 9% for December 31, 2026) due to the absence of official initiation, congressional hurdles to treaty exit, and enduring US strategic interests in countering Russia and China. April discussions between President Trump and NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte yielded criticisms but no action, while Rutte recently noted improved transatlantic signals. An upcoming NATO summit could influence commitments.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jean-Luc Mélenchon's announcement on May 3 that he will seek the presidency for a fourth time as La France Insoumise leader marks the latest declaration amid France's fragmented political landscape, with President Macron term-limited after two terms. Conservatives selected Senate leader Bruno Retailleau as their candidate in late April via internal vote, while far-right Rassemblement National eyes Jordan Bardella or Marine Le Pen, and centrists favor former premiers Édouard Philippe or Gabriel Attal per recent polls. The left remains divided without a unified primary, complicating signature collection from 500 elected officials across 30 departments required for ballot access. Upcoming 2026 municipal elections will aid parrainages and test party strength ahead of the April 2027 first round.

markets.question: Will Shake Shack (SHAK) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Shake Shack is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Shake Shack’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.12 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Shake Shack reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.12 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Shake Shack releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Philippines vs Indonesia - Toss Match Double Philippines Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Philippines and Indonesia scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

Philippines enter with strong recent form, winning four of their last five T20Is against Indonesia—including a dominant 78-run victory on December 11, 2025—and boasting a W L W W W W streak, while Indonesia slumped to a loss against Malaysia on May 1 amid an L L W L L L run. Absent pitch reports or weather updates, teams lack clear preference for batting or bowling first, keeping the pre-match ritual tightly contested.

markets.question: Baltimore Orioles vs. Miami Marlins: O/U 10.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Baltimore Orioles and Miami Marlins combine to score 11 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 11, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 5 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from May 4 - 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a recent lull in global seismicity, with no magnitude 6.5 or greater earthquakes recorded by USGS since the M7.4 off Japan on April 20—over two weeks ago—positioning zero events at 54% implied probability for May 4-10. This quiet period follows two M7.4s in early April but aligns with USGS long-term averages of 15-20 such events annually worldwide (roughly 0.3-0.4 per week), where Poisson variability often yields null weeks. No seismic swarms, foreshock sequences, or strain accumulation on major subduction zones or transform faults currently signal elevated risk. A M6.0 struck the Philippines on May 4, but fell short of the moment magnitude threshold; real-time USGS feeds through May 10 could shift odds with new detections.

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio visit China by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
If Marco Rubio visits China by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Rubio physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of China. Whether or not Rubio enters Chinese airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from Marco Rubio, the US Federal Government, and the Chinese government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Qualifiers concluded in late March with inter-confederation playoffs affirming continental hierarchies, while the group draw looms without major injury disruptions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 28°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Recent Ukrainian tactical gains on the Vasylivka front in Zaporizhzhia Oblast have shaped trader consensus, with forces infiltrating the southern part of nearby Prymorske over the past week amid ongoing Russian offensive efforts.** As of early May 2026, Ukrainian drone strikes disrupted Russian supply lines near Novoyakovlivka and Pavlivka, enabling full recapture of those positions in late April and complicating ground advances toward Vasylivka, which remains under Ukrainian control. Institute for the Study of War reports indicate Russian troops prioritizing assaults in the adjacent Hulyaipole direction while facing counterattacks, reflecting broader patterns of incremental Russian territorial gains slowed by logistics vulnerabilities. With the May 31 resolution approaching, key factors include potential reinforcements, weather impacts on mobility, and diplomatic signals on Western military aid, maintaining a closely contested outlook.

markets.question: Map Handicap: MOUZ (-1.5) vs Gentle Mates (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between MOUZ and Gentle Mates in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ wins 2 or more maps than Gentle Mates in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gentle Mates". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: MOUZ (-1.5) vs Gentle Mates (+1.5)

events.title: Counter-Strike: MOUZ vs Gentle Mates (BO3) - PGL Astana Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between MOUZ and Gentle Mates in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ win the match against Gentle Mates.

This market will resolve to "Gentle Mates" if Gentle Mates win the match against MOUZ.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: MOUZ enters as the clear trader consensus favorite against Gentle Mates in PGL Astana 2026 Swiss group stage round 1 BO3, backed by their #9 world ranking, perfect 2-0 head-to-head record including a dominant ESL Pro League S22 win, and consistent top-10 finishes like 3rd at PGL Cluj-Napoca. Recent roster shakeup benched Jimpphat and Brollan for newcomers jL and xelex just 17 days ago, contributing to mixed form with losses to Spirit and FURIA offset by wins over Aurora and Legacy. Lower-ranked #35 Gentle Mates shows qualifier momentum, winning three of five recent BO3s against mid-tier foes like FOKUS and 9INE, but struggled versus G2 and RED Canids; map edges favor them on Inferno (82% win rate) while MOUZ excels on Mirage (73%) and Overpass (100%), setting up key veto battles in this high-stakes opener where a win advances 1-0 in Swiss.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold (GC) futures trade near $4,690 per ounce, retracing from January 2026's $5,595 all-time high amid sticky inflation and resilient U.S. economic data that have curbed aggressive Federal Reserve rate cut bets. March CPI surged to 3.3% annually—the highest since May 2024—pushing 10-year Treasury yields to 4.35% and supporting a U.S. dollar index around 98, both pressuring the non-yielding asset lower recently. Countervailing tailwinds include declining real yields, robust central bank gold purchases from emerging markets, and elevated geopolitical risks. Traders focus on April CPI release May 12, May nonfarm payrolls, and the June 16-17 FOMC for signals on the policy path, with futures implying limited easing ahead.

markets.question: Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both Denmark and the United States sign a deal, treaty, or similar international agreement of any kind relating to Greenland by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any U.S.–Danish agreement relating to Greenland will qualify, regardless of subject matter, including but not limited to sovereignty, governance, security arrangements, or access to natural resources.

Examples of qualifying deals include but are not limited to a treaty that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession (even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement treaty establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission; or agreements permitting additional U.S. troop stationing, basing access, or resource extraction rights in Greenland.

This market will resolve to “Yes” only if a qualifying agreement is formally signed by authorized representatives of both Denmark and the United States. Official announcements, statements of intent, or declarations that an agreement has been reached will not suffice unless accompanied by signatures from both sides. Whether or not a qualifying deal is later passed by the respective parliaments or enters into force will not affect this market’s resolution. Signaling from Greenland’s population will not be considered.

Announcements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or understandings that are not formally signed by both parties will not qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction, control, basing rights, or access arrangements in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying agreements.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 91.5% implied probability against a Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30, driven by Denmark's and Greenland's unwavering insistence that sovereignty remains non-negotiable. Trump's January 2026 revival of acquisition ambitions—initially backed by tariff threats on NATO allies and military rhetoric—prompted massive protests in Nuuk, European solidarity with Copenhagen, and early Danish elections in March amid public backlash. While Davos talks led to plans updating the 1951 defense agreement for expanded US military access and barring Chinese/Russian minerals exploitation, no sovereignty-transferring pact has materialized. Recent US military expansion bids in April face ongoing resistance, leaving scant time for formal signing amid diplomatic entanglements.

markets.question: Set Handicap: Eala (-1.5) vs Wang (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Alexandra Eala and Xinyu Wang in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Eala" if Alexandra Eala wins by 2 or more sets than Xinyu Wang, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Wang."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Xinyu Wang vs Alexandra Eala
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Xinyu Wang and Alexandra Eala in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Xinyu Wang' if Xinyu Wang advances against Alexandra Eala.

This market will resolve to 'Alexandra Eala' if Alexandra Eala advances against Xinyu Wang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Alexandra Eala at 53% implied probability in this Internazionali BNL d'Italia second-round clash on Rome's red clay, reflecting a competitive balance between the WTA No. 42's rising momentum and No. 30 Xinyu Wang's experience. Eala advanced with a gritty 6-0, 3-6, 6-4 win over Magdalena Frech in the opener, showcasing resilience after her career-high ranking climb, while Wang enters rested via a seeding bye but off a recent clay round-of-16 loss to Alina Charaeva at the Catalonia Open. Their Auckland hard-court head-to-head favors Wang 1-0 in a three-set semifinal thriller, yet clay surface history—Wang's modest 4-3 record this year versus Eala's building form—creates uncertainty. Late injury reports or weather delays could shift odds, as both appear fit with no withdrawals noted.

Trader consensus prices in frontrunners' momentum, head-to-head edges, and roster stability amid patch meta shifts, with upsets possible in remaining matches.

Injuries loom large—Corinthians sidelined by muscular issues to Yuri Alberto, Hugo, and Felipe Longo; Santa Fe without Franco Fagúndez and Ewil Murillo—ahead of the first leg at Neo Química Arena on April 15 that could shift group dynamics and probabilities.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 74.5% implied probability against another major US sovereign credit rating downgrade before 2027, reflecting Fitch Ratings' April 30, 2026 affirmation of its AA+ stable outlook despite projecting US debt-to-GDP at 122% by year-end—up from 116.6% in 2025—amid widening fiscal deficits averaging $1.9 trillion annually per CBO estimates. This stability follows Moody's Aa1 downgrade in May 2025 and prior S&P/Fitch actions, with agencies citing chronic governance gridlock but no acute triggers like debt ceiling default risks, as the $41.1 trillion limit provides headroom above current $39 trillion levels. Key catalysts include FY2027 budget talks and potential debt limit hikes, where bipartisan resolutions have historically averted crises, underscoring the resilience of US Treasuries backed by global reserve demand.

markets.question: Will Vivek Ramaswamy win the Ohio Governor Republican Primary Election by between 50% and 60%?
markets.description:
The Republican Primary for Governor of Ohio is currently scheduled to take place on May 5, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including those from the Ohio Republican Party.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vivek Ramaswamy's landslide victory in yesterday's Ohio Republican gubernatorial primary, securing 82% of the vote to Casey Putsch's 18% for a 65-point margin, has locked in trader consensus at 99% odds for the 60-70% outcome on Polymarket. Trump endorsement, Ohio GOP backing, and Ramaswamy's $40 million self-funding fueled pre-election polls showing 76% support, which materialized amid low-turnout conditions and a thinned field after Heather Hill's ballot removal. With certified results confirming the blowout, only an improbable recount challenging thousands of votes could shift the margin into another bin, though such scenarios remain negligible given the gap.

markets.question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Zizou Bergs vs Terence Atmane

This market will resolve to 'Zizou Bergs' if Zizou Bergs advances against Terence Atmane.

This market will resolve to 'Terence Atmane' if Terence Atmane advances against Zizou Bergs.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Both enter atop parallel clay momentum: Bergs reached the Aix-en-Provence Challenger final last week after a Madrid Masters opening-round upset over Marin Cilic, posting a 9-10 YTD mark at No. 39; Atmane advanced to Madrid's third round, gutting out cramps for a comeback win over Ugo Humbert before falling to Alexander Zverev, holding No. 51. Even rankings and baseline prowess create parity, but Atmane's recent physical struggles could tip toward Bergs if recovery falters, while lefty serving or fatigue from Bergs' deeper run might swing it Atmane's way on Rome's slower courts.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

Key catalysts include April CPI data on May 12 and summer FOMC sessions, which could alter the policy path if inflation reaccelerates.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, which produced a hung National Assembly with no bloc reaching the 46 seats needed for a majority, outgoing Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement (29 seats) failed to secure coalition partners by April 20, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline nominating a candidate after initial consultations. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (28 seats), allied with the NSi-SLS-Fokus bloc (9 seats) and Democrats (6 seats) at 43 seats total, has advanced talks for a minority government, bolstered by a May 5 announcement of agreed coalition guidelines and parliamentary approval of an SDS-proposed reduction in ministries from 19 to 14 via 49 votes. Resni.ca (5 seats) external support could enable investiture; second-round presidential consultations this week may yield a Janša mandate, with a vote to follow amid risks of snap elections if unsuccessful.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gainare Tottori holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at home in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West B following their commanding 3-1 away win over Reilac Shiga on April 29, snapping a prior losing streak and highlighting defensive vulnerabilities in Shiga's backline during recent matches like a 2-0 loss to Sagan Tosu. Tottori's momentum from that head-to-head result, combined with Axis Bird Stadium home advantage, drives their 46.5% implied probability, while Shiga's mid-table position (7th) and struggles to keep clean sheets keep their away win viable at 37.5%. The tight odds reflect a closely contested matchup, with draw pricing underscoring frequent stalemates in prior encounters and both teams' inconsistent form. No major injury updates alter lineups.

markets.question: Anysphere (Cursor) IPO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors AI chipmaker Cerebras (99.7% implied probability) and SpaceX (94.5%) for IPOs before year-end 2026, propelled by Cerebras' April 17 S-1 filing and recent prospectus update targeting a mid-May debut at a $26-35 billion valuation amid explosive demand for AI inference hardware rivaling Nvidia. SpaceX's early-April confidential SEC submission and planned early-June roadshow further solidify its lead, fueled by Starship milestones and massive capital needs for satellite constellations. Anthropic (66%) and Discord (63%) trail on AI growth pressures and January filings, respectively, while OpenAI odds dipped to 29% after CFO signals of revenue shortfalls pushing timelines to 2027. Upcoming catalysts include Cerebras pricing and SpaceX roadshow, though regulatory reviews or market volatility could introduce delays in this capital-hungry tech landscape.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jakarta be 30°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/id/jakarta/WIHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus splits nearly evenly between 34-35°C and 31-33°C for Jakarta's May 8 high temperature, driven by conflicting signals from official BMKG forecasts and global models amid the early dry season transition. BMKG's May 5 update projects clear skies (cerah) across DKI Jakarta with highs of 30-31°C, high humidity (65-95%), and low rain risk, tilting toward lower brackets. Yet, recent days' observed peaks near 34°C—exceeding prior forecasts—plus ECMWF and GFS ensembles showing stronger solar heating and minimal convection, support 34-35°C via urban heat island amplification in the city center. Differentiating factor: afternoon sea breeze or isolated thunderstorms capping temps below 33°C versus prolonged insolation. Next BMKG advisory expected May 6 could resolve uncertainty.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: T1's competitive 6-3 record in the LCK 2026 Spring Split, placing them third behind dominant Hanwha Life Esports and KT Rolster, balances trader sentiment on roster stability versus adjustment needs, with Yes at 53% reflecting Doran's contract expiring at season's end amid the team's recovery from an early 3-3 slump. Long-term deals—Faker to 2029, Oner and Peyz to 2028, Keria to 2029—anchor the core, reinforced by recent staff tweaks like Easyhoon's coaching addition following kkOma's temporary leave. Prolonged struggles or a shallow playoff exit could spur mid-split benching or top-lane swap before July's Summer lock; conversely, a deep run would solidify the lineup per T1's history of international peaks despite domestic inconsistencies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2026 Women's US Open winner reflects a tightly contested hard-court hierarchy, with Aryna Sabalenka's world No. 1 ranking and WTA 1000 triumphs at Indian Wells and Miami giving her a slight edge over Elena Rybakina, the Australian Open champion who ousted her in that Grand Slam final after dismantling Iga Swiatek in the quarters. Swiatek trails at third in rankings post-Madrid, her clay prowess less translatable to the US Open's outdoor hard courts despite consistent top finishes. The race stays bunched amid breakouts like Victoria Mboko's deep runs at Indian Wells and Miami, Xiyu Wang's early-2026 upset streak, and Elise Mertens' steady baseline game, underscoring a deep field with no runaway favorite ahead of the summer hard-court swing.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 180-199 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley at 48% implied probability as the lead underwriter for SpaceX's anticipated mid-2026 IPO, driven by its longstanding advisory role with Elon Musk—including leading Tesla's 2010 public debut and financing the Twitter acquisition—bolstered by December 2025 Reuters reporting naming it the frontrunner. Recent Financial Times and Reuters disclosures in January and April 2026 revealed SpaceX assembling a massive 21-bank syndicate code-named Project Apex, prominently featuring Morgan Stanley alongside Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (16.2%), reflecting competitive jockeying amid valuation talks exceeding $1.5 trillion. No single lead has been confirmed, but historical precedents and ongoing preparations keep the race tight, with resolution hinging on final syndicate announcements ahead of a potential Nasdaq listing.

markets.question: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

In February 2026, a House Oversight Committee deposition saw Wexner, Epstein's longtime financial patron, deny knowledge of the sex offender's crimes, claiming he was duped; lawmakers questioned his credibility but took no further prosecutorial steps. markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

A realistic shift could arise from rapid Ukraine peace breakthroughs prompting a neutral-site summit in Turkey, Switzerland, or a Gulf state, though no such events are scheduled before the deadline.

markets.question: Will France send warships through the Strait of Hormuz by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a national government, its military, or a broad consensus of credible reporting confirms that the listed country's warships transited through the Strait of Hormuz between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "warship transit" is defined as a military vessel passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Military cargo or support vessels will be considered “warships”; however, commercial or civilian vessels will not qualify.

For the purposes of this market, only transits through the Strait of Hormuz will be considered, defined as passage through the narrowest portion of the waterway between Iran and Oman. Operations solely in the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea without passage through this narrowest section will not qualify.

Official confirmation by a national government or its military that its vessels transited through the Strait of Hormuz during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that such a transit occurred during the specified timeframe will also suffice.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as official announcements that a country has deployed naval vessels to transit or escort shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Confirmations referring only to naval presence in the broader region, including the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea, without confirmed transit through the Strait itself, as well as aerial operations, cyber operations, or actions by proxies or third parties, will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the respective national governments or their militaries; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis triggered by Iran's blockade following U.S.-Israeli strikes, trader consensus on Polymarket reflects U.S. naval dominance, with confirmed transits by destroyers like USS Truxtun and USS Mason in April, escorting merchant vessels under Project Freedom as recently as May 4—driving Brent crude volatility from $112 highs to $97.48 on de-escalation hopes after Trump's pause announcement. Elevated war risk insurance for tankers at 2-6% of vessel value underscores supply disruption fears for 20% of global oil flows, amplifying energy sector risk premia. UK and France plan deployments amid stalled talks, with May 31 resolution hinging on Iran-U.S. negotiations and potential multinational patrols.

markets.question: Will Santos Matheus Cunha win the 2026 PFA Player of the Year?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year.

If two or more players are announced as winners of the 2026 PFA Players’ Player of the Year award, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no 2025-2026 PFA Players' Player of the Year winner is declared by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Professional Footballers’ Association; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruno Fernandes commands 56% trader consensus for PFA Players' Player of the Year, driven by his Premier League-leading 19 assists and 109 chances created, captaining Manchester United's resurgence up the table with vital goals and creativity in recent fixtures. Declan Rice holds 38.5% as Arsenal's midfield enforcer, pivotal in their title contention and Champions League final berth, though trailing Fernandes' output. Erling Haaland's prolific 24-goal tally earns 6.3%, but lags in playmaking amid Manchester City's inconsistent form. Fernandes overtook Rice as favorite in late April following standout performances, including PFA Fans' Player of the Month for March, underscoring player-voted esteem for his all-action dominance.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol remains in custody following his February 2026 conviction and life sentence for insurrection tied to the 2024 martial law declaration, with an appeals court on April 29 increasing his term to seven years for obstruction of arrest. These severe penalties, upheld amid multiple detention warrant extensions, reflect judicial consensus on charges from the political crisis that led to his impeachment and ouster by the Constitutional Court in April 2025. Absent successful higher appeals or extraordinary pardons—unlikely given the timeline to December 31, 2026—traders price a 92.5% implied probability of "No," emphasizing entrenched legal barriers over release scenarios.

markets.question: Will Milwaukee Brewers win the 2026 NL Central title
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB National League Central division.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the team whose listed nickname comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to be named the 2026 National League Central division champion per the rules of the MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Cubs lead the NL Central at 24-12, their blistering .667 winning percentage and 16-5 home record fueling trader consensus at 58% implied probability for the 2026 division crown, bolstered by a recent seven-game winning streak capped by a 3-2 extra-innings victory over the Reds. Despite multiple players on the injured list, the Cubs' pitching rotation and timely hitting have created separation from pursuers, including the Cardinals (21-14), Reds (20-16), Pirates (19-17), and Brewers (18-16), all hovering above .500 in a dominant division with a collective .600 win rate. Milwaukee's relative struggles despite defending status, coupled with Cincinnati's losses to division foes, widen the Cubs' edge in playoff odds projections nearing 93%.

markets.question: Spread: Gwangju FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Gwangju FC" if Gwangju FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gangwon FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gwangju FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gangwon's recent dominance over Gwangju FC, including a 3-0 victory in their earlier K League 1 matchup this season and an unbeaten streak across the last six head-to-heads, underpins trader consensus favoring the visitors despite Gwangju's home advantage at Gwangju Football Stadium. Gwangju languish in 12th place after 10 matches with just six points and a league-worst -16 goal difference, mired in a five-game losing skid marked by defensive frailties (23 goals conceded). Gangwon sit comfortably 6th on 13 points, buoyed by solid recent form (wins in three of last five) and tactical adjustments fueling their mid-table push, though Gwangju's desperation for points could spark an upset in this relegation scrap.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (HIGH) $160 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares closed at $138.86 on May 5, reflecting a modest 2.31% year-to-date gain amid building anticipation for Q1 2026 earnings on May 7, where Wall Street anticipates $0.30 EPS—a 25% year-over-year increase—and $2.62 billion in revenue, up 15%. Recent momentum stems from Oppenheimer's upgrade to Outperform with a $180 price target, citing materializing organic revenue drivers like travel services scaling and prospective 2026 World Cup tailwinds, against a consensus analyst target of $153. Q4 2025 delivered revenue beats despite EPS misses, with full-year guidance signaling low-teens growth acceleration. Traders eye nights-and-seats booked and EBITDA margins for directional cues, as earnings reaction could spur May volatility around key price thresholds.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 74-75°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance for Houston shows a cold front sweeping through late May 6 into May 7, bringing scattered showers, thunderstorms, and persistent cloud cover that caps highs in the mid-70s°F, driving trader consensus to 74-75°F at 40.5% and 76-77°F at 24.5% implied probabilities. Light northeasterly winds post-frontal boundary will enhance onshore flow, suppressing temperatures below May's climatological average of 86°F amid an upper-level trough. Recent developments include heavy rainfall and flooding on May 1, followed by subdued highs like 71°F on May 5, reinforcing the cooler pattern; NOAA model ensembles (GFS, ECMWF) converge on similar outcomes, though afternoon storm intensity could tweak peak heating—monitor evening forecast discussions for updates ahead of official airport observations resolving the market.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Wilson's history of 55-60% general election margins against weak Democratic opposition sustains low 18% odds for Democrats, whose primary features four underfunded contenders—Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief—plus independent Dayna Alane Smith (Workers Party). Candidate-specific outcomes A, B, and Other trade near 50¢ on low volume amid pre-primary uncertainty for the November 4 general election.

markets.question: Will Trump agree to Iranian enrichment of uranium by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States agrees to the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Continued enrichment of uranium by Iran refers to US acceptance of the enrichment of, or the right to enrich, any quantity of uranium by Iran for any future amount of time. Agreements that include limitations, restrictions, or specified terms (e.g., caps on enrichment level, monitoring requirements) will qualify, provided the United States accepts continued enrichment.

The United States will be considered to have agreed to the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran if:

- Donald Trump or another authorized representative of the Government of the United States publicly announces that they have definitively agreed to accept the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran.
- Continued enrichment of uranium by Iran is included as part of a treaty or deal that is formally established between the United States and Iran, either through signing or other formal means.

Agreement refers to an explicit acceptance, authorization or consent to the specified action. Only announcements of definitive agreement will qualify. Suggestions, negotiations, expressions of openness, or other non-definitive statements will not qualify.

Any definitive agreement or commitment made before the resolution date will be considered, regardless of when or whether the specified action is begun.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Donald Trump and the US government and their official representatives; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used to verify the details of an announcement or formal agreement.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent pause on "Project Freedom" naval operations in the Strait of Hormuz, announced May 6, reflects optimism from "very good talks" with Iran over the past 24 hours, as Tehran reviews a new US proposal mediated through Pakistan. This follows a fragile ceasefire since early April after US and Israeli strikes, with prior Iranian 10-point offers rejected for deferring nuclear constraints. Trump insists Iran has conceded it cannot pursue nuclear weapons, eyeing a memorandum on uranium transfers, sanctions relief, and Hormuz reopening before his China trip. Traders assess leverage from maximum pressure policy against Tehran's economic strain, with May 31 deadline looming amid risks of escalation if demands like full sanctions lift remain unmet.

markets.question: Agut vs. Maestrelli: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Roberto Bautista Agut and Francesco Maestrelli in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Roberto Bautista Agut vs Francesco Maestrelli
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Roberto Bautista Agut and Francesco Maestrelli in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Roberto Bautista Agut' if Roberto Bautista Agut advances against Francesco Maestrelli.

This market will resolve to 'Francesco Maestrelli' if Francesco Maestrelli advances against Roberto Bautista Agut.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Roberto Bautista Agut, the 38-year-old Spaniard ranked No. 89 and retiring at season's end, faces Italian wild card Francesco Maestrelli (No. 112) in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first-round clash on Rome's outdoor clay. Bautista Agut enters off mixed clay results—retiring early versus Berrettini in Monte Carlo, a straight-sets loss to Tirante in Madrid, but a Challenger win over Gueymard Wayenburg before falling to Tabilo in Aix-en-Provence last week—showcasing grit amid form dips. Maestrelli, buoyed by home-crowd support at Foro Italico, arrives winless in recent clay outings, including qualifying defeats in Madrid and Monte Carlo plus early Challenger exits. With no head-to-head and Bautista Agut's superior Masters 1000 experience on the surface, traders weigh veteran pedigree against the underdog's motivation and upset potential in this bottom-half matchup.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.
